purpose: Classifies emerging markets into two clusters with opposite responses to US fiscal transmission, enabling differentiated risk assessment and positioning
components: Cluster A: surplus commodity exporters (Brazil, Gulf, Indonesia) — Kalecki beneficiaries
Cluster B: deficit food/energy importers (Egypt, Pakistan, Turkey, Kenya, Tunisia) — doom loop at 5-10x DM speed
Agricultural price → sovereign CDS indicator (operates through ~12-15 managed-FX food-subsidy nations)
CEE rearmament channel (Poland 4%+ GDP defense as fiscal-expansion-currency-positive)
DOGE USAID withdrawal as disproportionate Cluster B shock
current_assessment: Bifurcation is closer to current observation than forecast. Brazil vs Egypt during 2022 wheat spike provides concrete evidence. Cluster B accounts for <20% of EM GDP but >60% of EM sovereign stress events. The 5-10x DM speed claim is directionally supported but the range spans a 45x variance in its own evidence.
limitations: 5-10x speed differential is an extremely wide range
Classification may be too binary for countries with mixed characteristics
Commodity price cycles could temporarily reverse cluster performance
The model does not account for idiosyncratic domestic political factors
